titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Love Makes a Family Records
titlestmt.author: Compiled by Mary Caldera

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday April 17, 2021 at 08:58
Updated to enhance description of computer files.

did.abstract: The Love Makes a Family (LMF) records consist of email correspondence, bylaws, reports, meeting minutes, research data, publications, Web pages, social media account files, topical files, interviews and testimonies, photographs, audiovisual recordings, and newspaper clippings documenting the history, structure, and activities of LMF, Inc. and its related organizations, the LMF Political Action Committee (PAC) and the LMF Foundation. LMF's principal goals were to pass a second-parent adoption law; support efforts to pass a domestic partnership package for state employees; defeat Defense of Marriage Amendments (DOMAs) both to the state statute and the state constitution; and pass a marriage equality law for same-sex couples in Connecticut. As the first three goals were reached by 2000, the records primarily document LMF's efforts on behalf of marriage equality.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of Love Makes a Family, 2009 and 2010.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Love Makes a Family (LMF) records consist of email correspondence, bylaws, reports, meeting minutes, research data, publications, Web pages, social media account files, topical files, interviews and testimonies, photographs, audiovisual recordings, and newspaper clippings documenting the history, structure, and activities of LMF, Inc. and its related organizations, the LMF Political Action Committee (PAC) and the LMF Foundation. The records are comprised primarily of the records maintained by Anne Stanback (president and executive director) and Carol Buckheit (associate director and executive director) and document all aspects of the organization including executive administration, strategy and policy development, fundraising, education and advocacy efforts, communications, and relationships to other marriage equailty organizations. As LMF reached its first three goals in its first year, the vast majority of the records document the effort to legalize same-sex marriage. The records document all the major developments of the marriage equality movement in Connecticut, as well as the broader national movement and its opposition. Additionally individual lesbian, gay, bisexual, and transgendered persons' lives and the impact of marriage discrimination is richly documented in testimonials, public hearing statements, and interviews. To a lesser extent other lesbian, gay, bisexual, and transgender anti-discrimination efforts in the state are also documented.
Dates for computer files were determined from the files' last modified dates and donor supplied information.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Series I, Box 20 is closed to research until January 1, 2025. Box 21 requires written permission to access from the Love Makes a Family trustees until January 1, 2025, after which they are open to research. Copies of commercially produced audiovisual materials contained in this collection cannot be made for researcher use outside of the repository. Original computer files may not be accessed due to their fragility. Researchers must consult access copies.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The records are arranged in three series: I. Administrative Records,1991-2010. II. Audiovisual Recordings and Photographs, 2000-2009. III. Website and Social Media Sites, 2009-2010.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Love Makes a Family (LMF) (1999-2009) was a Connecticut statewide coalition of organizations and individuals whose mission was to pass a second-parent adoption law; support efforts to pass a domestic partnership package for state employees; defeat Defense of Marriage Amendments (DOMAs) both to the state statute and the state constitution; and to pass a marriage equality law for same-sex couples in Connecticut. The first three goals were reached by 2000; the marriage equality law was passed in 2008. In 2005, LMF joined a statewide coalition to end discrimination based on gender identity and expression, thereby adding transgender rights to its awareness and advocacy campaign. Passing a marriage bill, however, remained its primary focus. Love Makes a Family developed into three inter-related entities: LMF, Inc., incorporated as a 501(c)(4) in 2000; the LMF Political Action Committee (PAC), established in 2002; and the LMF Foundation, incorporated as a 501(c)(3) in 2006. After meeting its goals through community education, grassroots organizing, and legislative advocacy and lobbying, LMF, Inc. and LMF Foundation disbanded in 2009. The LMF PAC continues to work for lesbian, gay, bisexual, and transgender equality in Connecticut by supporting and working for pro equality candidates for elected office.

In its first phase of work on second-parent adoption during 1999 and 2000, LMF Inc.'s informal Steering Committee was comprised of representatives from five statewide organizations: the American Civil Liberties Union of Connecticut, the Connecticut Coalition of LGBT Civil Rights, the Connecticut Conference of the United Church of Christ, the Connecticut State Permanent Commission on the Status of Women, and the Connecticut Women's Education and Legal Fund. Once LMF, Inc. began the process of organizational incorporation and refocused its mission on marriage equality, the Steering Committee expanded, consisting of a president, vice-president, secretary, treasurer; representatives from six regional chapters; and representatives from six statewide organizations (the Permanent Commission on the Status of Women stepped down and Parents, Friends and Families of Lesbians and Gays (PFLAG), True Colors and DemocracyWorks ultimately joined). This structure remained in place until 2006 when a more traditional Board of Directors structure was put in place. In 2006, the LMF Foundation was incorporated as a 501(c) (3) that was primarily run by a board of directors, an executive director, and an associate director. LMF, Inc. was originally run by volunteers but by 2008, it had grown to consist of several full and part-time staff, including administrators, field organizers, a lobbyist, a financial manager, an events coordinator, and a Web site designer. While the organization benefited from the efforts of many individuals, several stand out for their length of service: Anne Stanback served as president from 2000 to 2005 and executive director from 2006-2009; Betty Gallo served as lobbyist from 1999 to 2005 and 2007 to 2008; Alice Pritchard served in various roles from 2002 to 2008; Beverley Brakeman served as secretary from 2003 to 2007; Chelsea Turner served as a lobbyist from 2000 to 2004; and Carol Buckheit served in various roles, including associate director and executive director, respectively, from 2005 to 2009.

The organization accomplished its goals through community education, grassroots organizing, and legislative advocacy and lobbying. LMF volunteers and staff trained and organized community volunteers; gave presentations; organized community forums, rallies, and public hearings; canvassed voters; commissioned research; managed a speakers' bureau; served as a clearinghouse on civil union and marriage information; and lobbied legislators. LMF was recognized as a leader in the marriage equality movement. Anne Stanback and LMF were recognized with several honors, including Stanback's induction into the Connecticut Women's Hall of Fame.
Information in this organizational history is drawn from the Love Makes a Family Annual Report (2001-2008).

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Love Makes a Family Records (MS 1962). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright has been transferred to Yale University for unpublished materials authored or otherwise produced by the creator(s) of this collection. Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Computer files in this collection were captured from Love Makes a Family networks and social media accounts by Manuscripts and Archives staff in 2010 for preservation and access. Additional information about actions taken may be available at the repository. Computer files acquired on media was accessioned by the Yale University Digital Accessioning Support Service (DASS) in 2019. Born digital archival material acquired on media by Yale University Library repositories is accessioned through the DASS prior to researcher use. For more information on the DASS and the actions taken during its accessioning work, click here: Born Digital @ Yale: Digital Accessioning Support Service [https://guides.library.yale.edu/c.php?g=300384&p=3593184].
Original computer file structure maintained and titles retained, except for a few file names that were modified for clarity. Duplicate files were deleted.

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: CDs and DVDs (boxes 30u, 64u, 67u, 69u, 70u, 72u,77u2, 78u,82u,85u,89u, 92u-94u) duplicated content elsewhere in the collection and have been removed from the collection.

corpname.part: Love Makes a Family Political Action Committee

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series I is arranged by administrative function.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
The Administrative Records series is divided into seven sections: Executive Director's records, Associate Director's records, Field Organizer's records, Religious Organizer's records, Financial records, Printed material, and General resource files.
The most substantive materials are found in the Executive Director's records, which document all aspects of the organization. For example, the organization's history can be gleaned from bylaws, board minutes, and annual reports. Lobbying, research, and advocacy activities pertaining to the same-sex marriage bill and same-sex marriage cases in various states can be found in the topical files under Legislative efforts. The Associate Director was primarily responsible for public relations and communications and, as such, records on those topics, including press releases and materials documenting fundraising events, are found in that section.

The records of the Field and Religious organizers provide a glimpse of LMF's field and specialized campaigns to gain support for their legislative agenda. The Financial records consist of budgets, audits, and tax returns. Of special note in the Printed material section are newspaper clippings covering the same-sex marriage movement in Connecticut and LMF's role in it from 2002 to 2009 and the "Love Makes A Family: The LMF Testimonial Book" with personal statements from state residents. Materials in the General resource files are of a more general nature and did not belong to any one person at LMF.

Many of the events documented in Series I are also documented in Series II, Audiovisual Recordings and Photographs. Researchers interested in a particular event should consult the listings for both series.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Original computer files may not be accessed due to their fragility. Researchers must consult access copies.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Original computer files, as well as preservation masters, are restricted. Electronic files cannot be accessed remotely. Copies of the files are available in the repository.

did.unittitle: Lawrence v. Texas Supreme Court Decision

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes planning documents related to LMF's decision to close.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Public Act 02-105 granted limited rights to same-sex couples.

userestrict.p: Original computer files, as well as preservation masters, are restricted. Electronic files cannot be accessed remotely. Copies of the files are available in the repository.

accessrestrict.p: Consists of photocopies of a letter from Betty Hudson and newspaper clippings on Connecticut's anti-discrimination bill.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Written permission from Love Makes a Family trustees is required to access this material until January 1, 2025.

title.part: Marriage Makes a Word of Difference

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of notes on the the making of the film, a project of the LMF Foundation. The film was directed by Fran Rzeznik and produced by Carol Buckheit.

did.unittitle: Messaging and strategy from MAP (Movement Advancement Project)

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted until January 1, 2025.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of flyers, postcards, cards, invitations, brochures for various events, fundraisers, and forums.

did.unittitle: Talk by Carol Buckheit, "Media Advocacy and Community Organizing"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of material concerning Trans Plantations , a one-woman play starring Janis Astor del Valle about LGBT issues, including marriage equality, in the Latino/a community.

did.unittitle: Same-sex marriage, Connecticut Bar Association forum
"Volunteer Recruitment Materials Packet," National Gay and Lesbian Task Force

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series, consisting of audiovisual recordings and photographs, richly documents presentations, interviews, rallies, press conferences and other LMF events. It includes LMF produced films and copies of Judiciary Committee hearings on same-sex marriage.
Many of the events documented in Series II are also documented in Series I, Administrative Files. Researchers interested in a particular event should consult the listings for both series.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series II is arranged chronologically by event or publication date.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Original computer files and audiovisual materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be accessed due to their fragility. Researchers must consult access copies.
Copies of commercially produced audiovisual materials contained in this collection cannot be made for researcher use outside of the repository.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Original audiovisual materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be played due to their fragility. Researchers must consult access copies.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copies of commercially produced audiovisual materials contained in this collection cannot be made for researcher use outside of the repository.

accessrestrict.p: As a preservation measure, original materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be used. Digital access copies must be provided for use. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at mssa.assist@yale.edu to request access.

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of digital photographs maintained by LMF on a shared drive and on photograph management sites. The photographs within each folder are organized by event.

altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at mssa.assist@yale.edu to request access to the digital copy.

did.unittitle: Eat, Drink and Be Married gala, Westport, Connecticut
Rally for Marriage Equality at the Capitol in Hartford, Connecticut

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Original computer files may not be accessed due to their fragility. Researchers must consult access copies.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Website and Social Media Sites series is arranged in five sections: ConvioWebsite, Website (Final), Facebook, Twitter, and YouTube. The files, copies from the original sites, document LMF's public representation and interactions from circa 2004 to 2009. Of particular note are the ConvioWebsite files, which include copies of multiple versions of the Web site, emails to members, and action alerts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of copies of LMF Website content from Convio, LMF's Web service provider. The files consist of action alerts (AA); stylesheets (CSS); documents (DS); event calendar information (EC); LMF emails sent from Convio; forms; images; java script (JS); photo albums (PS); PageBuilder pages (PB); archived versions of each web page (PB versions); StoryBuilder content (SB); and surveys.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of Advocacy Action Alerts. Includes file "0000 Event Calendar Filename Key.xls" with links to each action alert.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of stylesheets for Website.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes documents that were included in the Website.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of Event Calendar files. Includes file "0000 Evenet Calendar Filename Key.xls" with links to each event, event date, and title.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of all LMF emails sent through Convio.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of javascript used in the Website.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of Covio hosted photograph albums.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of final (full) version of the LMF Website. The home page and the entire Website can be accessed by clicking on "home.html". Includes "0000 PageBuilder Pages Filename Key.xls" with links to each page.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of "archived" versions of the LMF Website.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of news clips and press releases. Includes "0000 Story Builder Filename Key.xls" with links to content, published date and title.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of the final LMF Website homepage.

accessrestrict.p: As a preservation measure, original materials may not be used. Digital access copies must be provided for use. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at mssa.assist@yale.edu to request access

accessrestrict.p: As a preservation measure, original materials may not be used. Digital access copies must be provided for use. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at mssa.assist@yale.edu to request access

scopecontent.p: Consists of moving image files added to YouTube and linked to the LMF website.
